key_insight: Strain-space hyperreduction can extend beyond homogenization to large-deformation solid mechanics, delivering much better speed-accuracy tradeoffs than displacement-space sampling methods.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast reduced-order simulation of nonlinear solids with parameterized boundary conditions and material behavior, which is valuable for design, optimization, and digital twins.
method: Use offline lifting fields to enforce arbitrary parameterized Dirichlet boundary conditions by construction.
Form strain-space versions of ECM, E3C, and EMSL, and compare them to displacement-space ECSW.
Hyperreduce large-deformation problems by sampling and weighting strain contributions rather than full displacement fields.
evidence_signal: On two hyperelastic benchmark problems, strain-space methods outperformed displacement-space ECSW, with E3C and EMSL reaching about 10,000x and 100,000x speedups while preserving high accuracy.
watch_item: Results depend on effective offline construction of boundary-consistent lifting fields and careful sampling setup, which may add implementation complexity.
paper_title: Efficient strain-space hyperreduction in large-deformation solid mechanics
